# Alby Lightning Browser Extension

> AI/LLM developer context for the Alby browser extension — a cross-browser WebExtension for Bitcoin Lightning, Nostr, and Liquid payments.

## Overview

Alby is an open-source browser extension (Chrome, Firefox, Brave) that provides Lightning Network, Nostr, and Liquid wallet functionality via a WebExtension architecture. Users manage wallets, make payments, authenticate with websites (LNOAuth), and interact with Nostr — all from the browser.

## Repository Structure

```
src/
  app/                        # React popup UI (options page, dashboard)
    components/               # Shared React components
    context/                  # React context providers (AppContext, i18n)
    hooks/                    # Custom React hooks
    icons/                    # SVG icon components
    router/                   # React Router configuration
    screens/                  # Page-level components (home, send, receive, settings)
    styles/                   # Global styles (Tailwind CSS)
    utils/                    # UI utility functions
  extension/
    background-script/        # Service worker (background page)
      actions/                # Message action handlers
      bitcoin/                # Bitcoin-specific logic (bip32, psbt)
      connectors/             # Payment connector implementations (LND, CLN, Lnbits, Eclair, etc.)
      db.ts                   # IndexedDB persistence layer
      events.ts               # Event handlers
      index.ts                # Service worker entry point
      liquid/                 # Liquid/Elements integration
      migrations/             # Schema migration scripts
      mnemonic/               # BIP39 mnemonic handling
      nostr/                  # Nostr protocol implementation (relay pool, keys, events)
      permissions.ts          # Extension permission management
      registerContentScript.ts
      router.ts               # Internal message routing
      state.ts                # Global state management
    content-script/           # Injected content scripts (page communication)
    inpage-script/            # Injected in-page script (window.webln, window.nostr)
    providers/
      alby/                   # Alby built-in account provider (custodial)
      liquid/                 # Liquid wallet provider
      nostr/                  # Nostr provider
      webbtc/                 # Web Bitcoin provider (core WebLN implementation)
      webln/                  # WebLN API provider
      postMessage.ts          # Page ↔ extension message bridge
      promiseQueue.ts         # Async message queue
      providerBase.ts         # Base provider class
  common/
    constants.ts              # Shared constants
    lib/                      # Shared libraries
    settings.ts               # User settings model
    utils/                    # Shared utility functions
  i18n/                       # Internationalization (i18next)
    i18nConfig.ts             # i18next configuration
    locales/                  # Translation JSON files (en, de, es, etc.)
  fixtures/                   # Mock data and test fixtures
  types.ts                    # Shared TypeScript type definitions
  manifest.json               # WebExtension manifest (MV3)
build-utils/
  ProviderInjectionPlugin.js  # Webpack plugin for provider injection
scripts/
  check-changed-translations.js
  remove-outdated-translations.js
static/                      # Static assets (icons, images)
doc/
  SETUP.md                   # Local development setup guide
  CONTRIBUTION.md            # Contribution guidelines
  CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md         # Code of Conduct
```

## Technology Stack

- **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Framework**: React (popup/options UI)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS + PostCSS
- **Build**: Webpack (with SWC via .swcrc)
- **Testing**: Jest + Playwright (E2E)
- **Package Manager**: Yarn (v1 or v2)
- **Browser API**: WebExtension Manifest V3
- **Linting**: ESLint (@typescript-eslint), Prettier
- **Commit Convention**: commitlint (Conventional Commits), enforced via husky

## Key Concepts

- **WebLN**: Browser API standard for Lightning payments (`window.webln.enable()`, `webln.sendPayment()`, `webln.signMessage()`)
- **Nostr**: Decentralized social protocol — the extension manages keys, relay connections, and event signing
- **Connectors**: Backend-agnostic payment connectors (LND, CLN, Lnbits, Eclair, BTCPay, Xapo, Phoenix, Mutiny) — each implements a common interface
- **Inpage Scripts**: Scripts injected into web pages exposing `window.webln` and `window.nostr` global APIs
- **Content Scripts**: Bridge between injected inpage scripts and the extension background service worker
- **LNOAuth**: Lightning-based authentication protocol (sign challenge with Lightning node key)
- **Museum Mode**: Read-only mode for viewing wallets without signing capabilities

## Build & Development

```bash
yarn install                   # Install dependencies (Node.js v16+)
yarn dev:chrome                # Development build for Chrome
yarn dev:firefox               # Development build for Firefox
yarn dev:brave                 # Development build for Brave
yarn test                      # Run Jest test suite
yarn test:e2e                  # Run Playwright E2E tests
yarn lint                      # Run ESLint
yarn format                    # Run Prettier
yarn build                     # Production build
```

## Code Style

- TypeScript strict mode enabled
- Conventional Commits enforced via commitlint (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, etc.)
- Branch naming: `feat/feature-name`, `fix/bug-name`
- Prettier for formatting (config in `.prettierrc.json`)
- ESLint with `@typescript-eslint/recommended`
- EditorConfig in `.editorconfig`

## Testing

- **Unit tests**: Jest (config in `jest.config.js`)
- **E2E tests**: Playwright (config in `playwright.config.ts`)
- **Test fixtures**: `src/fixtures/`
- Custom test environment: `jest.custom-test-environment.js`

## Important Constraints

- WebExtension Manifest V3 — no persistent background pages, use service workers
- Cross-browser compatibility (Chrome, Firefox, Brave) — test across browsers
- Inpage scripts run in isolated world — communication via `postMessage` bridge only
- Do not leak private keys or mnemonic to content/inpage scripts
- All sensitive operations must go through the background service worker
- Follow existing connector patterns when adding new payment providers
- i18n: all user-facing strings must go through the i18n system, never hardcoded
